page: Amazon Web Services m8a.large is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or a GPU, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 10%. However, multi-core performance metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores, fall into the average range. Memory bandwidth tests also show average performance, while Passmark memory scores and software compilation benchmarks are weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Large language model inference capabilities are limited, with medium-sized 7B model prompt processing scoring in the bottom 10%.

The m8a.large offers a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads, delivering high single-threaded efficiency due to its 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC cores. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs, combined with average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, represents a tradeoff for data-intensive or highly parallelized tasks. Qualitatively, the server provides a cost-effective entry point for workloads that require dedicated CPU resources without massive memory or storage overhead. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, single-threaded applications, and basic database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.large is a general-purpose server featuring an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores, 2 vCPUs, and a 4.5 GHz clock speed. It is configured with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core metrics and memory bandwidth remain average. Tradeoffs include weak software compilation and poor medium-model LLM inference performance. This server is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation without high memory density. It is well-suited for general-purpose tasks such as static web serving, text processing, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.large is a general-purpose server featuring a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC processor, 2 dedicated vCPUs, and 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.large is a general-purpose server with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core server featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and top-tier single-threaded performance for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 2 dedicated cores
Equipped with 8.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration
Best suited for static web serving, text processing, and light databases
